options.systemMessage:
You are an expert API testing engineer and debugger. Analyze the failed API test case and provide a clear explanation in Vietnamese.

You MUST respond with ONLY a raw JSON object — no markdown, no code fences, no text outside JSON.

Required JSON structure:
{
  "summaryVi": "<1-2 câu tóm tắt nguyên nhân thất bại bằng tiếng Việt>",
  "possibleCauses": ["<cause 1>", "<cause 2>"],
  "suggestedNextActions": ["<action 1>", "<action 2>"],
  "confidence": "high|medium|low",
  "model": "<model name>",
  "tokensUsed": 0
}

parameters.jsCode:
const { rawContent, endpoints, projectContext } = $input.first().json;

if (!rawContent) {
  throw new Error('SRS document has no text content. Please ensure the document has text before triggering analysis.');
}

const endpointBlock = endpoints.length > 0
  ? [
      '=== AVAILABLE API ENDPOINTS (from linked Test Suite) ===',
      endpoints.map(ep => `- [${ep.endpointId}] ${ep.method} ${ep.path}${ep.summary ? ' — ' + ep.summary : ''}`).join('\n'),
      'Map each requirement to the most relevant endpoint path when possible.'
    ].join('\n')
  : '=== API ENDPOINTS: none provided — skip endpoint mapping ===';

const systemPrompt = `You are an expert software requirements analyst specialized in extracting testable requirements from Software Requirements Specification (SRS) documents.
Your task is to:
1. Parse the SRS document and extract EVERY distinct requirement.
2. Classify each requirement by type: functional, non-functional, security, performance, or constraint.
3. Identify testable constraints (boundary values, expected status codes, validation rules).
4. Detect ambiguities and formulate clarification questions for unclear requirements.
5. Assign a confidence score (0.0–1.0) based on how clear and complete the requirement is.
6. Map requirements to API endpoints when endpoint context is available.

IMPORTANT OUTPUT RULES:
- Return ONLY valid JSON — no markdown fences, no commentary.
- Every requirement MUST have: requirementCode, title, description, type, testableConstraints, assumptions, ambiguities, confidenceScore.
- testableConstraints MUST be a JSON array string: [{"constraint": "...", "priority": "High|Medium|Low"}]
- assumptions and ambiguities MUST be JSON array strings: ["...", "..."]
- type MUST be one of: functional, non-functional, security, performance, constraint
- For each ambiguity, also include a clarificationQuestion entry in the top-level clarificationQuestions array.`;

const userPrompt = [
  `=== SRS DOCUMENT CONTENT ===`,
  rawContent.trim(),
  '',
  endpointBlock,
  projectContext ? `=== PROJECT CONTEXT ===\n${projectContext}` : '',
  `=== TASK ===`,
  `Extract all requirements from the SRS document above.`,
  `For each requirement provide:`,
  `- requirementCode: string (e.g. REQ-001, REQ-002, ...)`,
  `- title: string (concise title)`,
  `- description: string (full description)`,
  `- type: "functional" | "non-functional" | "security" | "performance" | "constraint"`,
  `- testableConstraints: JSON array string [{"constraint": "...", "priority": "High|Medium|Low"}]`,
  `- assumptions: JSON array string ["..."]`,
  `- ambiguities: JSON array string ["..."]`,
  `- confidenceScore: float 0.0–1.0`,
  `- mappedEndpointPath: string | null (match to provided endpoints if applicable)`,
  '',
  `Also provide a top-level clarificationQuestions array for every detected ambiguity:`,
  `- requirementCode: string (matches the requirement)`,
  `- ambiguitySource: string (the ambiguous text)`,
  `- question: string (clear question to ask the user)`,
  `- isCritical: boolean (true if this ambiguity BLOCKS test generation)`,
  `- suggestedOptions: JSON array string ["option1", "option2"] | null`,
  '',
  `Return JSON in this exact format:`,
  `{`,
  `  "requirements": [...],`,
  `  "clarificationQuestions": [...],`,
  `  "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile",`,
  `  "tokensUsed": 0`,
  `}`
].filter(Boolean).join('\n');

return [{ json: { systemPrompt, userPrompt } }];

const { requirementsToRefine } = $input.first().json;

const reqBlocks = requirementsToRefine.map((req, i) => {
  const answers = Array.isArray(req.userAnswers) && req.userAnswers.length > 0
    ? req.userAnswers.map((a, j) =>
        `  Q${j + 1}: ${a.question}\n  Ambiguity: ${a.ambiguitySource ?? 'N/A'}\n  User Answer: ${a.userAnswer}`
      ).join('\n\n')
    : '  (no clarification answers provided)';

  return [
    `--- Requirement ${i + 1}: ${req.requirementCode} ---`,
    `Original Description: ${req.originalDescription ?? ''}`,
    `Existing Testable Constraints: ${req.existingTestableConstraints ?? '[]'}`,
    `Existing Ambiguities: ${req.existingAmbiguities ?? '[]'}`,
    `Original Confidence Score: ${req.originalConfidenceScore ?? 0}`,
    ``,
    `User Clarification Answers:`,
    answers
  ].join('\n');
}).join('\n\n');

const systemPrompt = `You are an expert software requirements analyst. Your task is to REFINE existing software requirements based on user-provided clarification answers.\n\nFor each requirement:\n1. Incorporate the user answers into updated, more precise testable constraints.\n2. Remove ambiguities that have been resolved by the user answers.\n3. Produce a refinementSummary explaining what changed and why.\n4. Re-calculate the confidenceScore (0.0-1.0) - it should increase after clarification.\n\nIMPORTANT OUTPUT RULES:\n- Return ONLY valid JSON - no markdown fences, no commentary.\n- refinedConstraints MUST be a JSON array string: [{"constraint": "...", "priority": "High|Medium|Low"}]\n- refinedConfidenceScore MUST be a float 0.0-1.0, and typically HIGHER than the original.\n- Each refined requirement MUST include: requirementId, requirementCode, refinedConstraints, refinedConfidenceScore, refinementSummary.`;

const userPrompt = [
  `=== REQUIREMENTS TO REFINE ===`,
  reqBlocks,
  ``,
  `=== TASK ===`,
  `Refine each requirement using the user clarification answers above.`,
  `Return JSON in this exact format:`,
  `{`,
  `  "refinedRequirements": [`,
  `    {`,
  `      "requirementId": "<guid from input>",`,
  `      "requirementCode": "REQ-001",`,
  `      "refinedConstraints": "[{\\"constraint\\": \\"...\\", \\"priority\\": \\"High\\"}]",`,
  `      "refinedConfidenceScore": 0.95,`,
  `      "refinementSummary": "Explanation of what was clarified and updated."`,
  `    }`,
  `  ],`,
  `  "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile",`,
  `  "tokensUsed": 0`,
  `}`
].join('\n');

return [{ json: { systemPrompt, userPrompt } }];

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { accepted: true, mode: 'async-callback', testSuiteId: $json.testSuiteId, endpointCount: $json.endpoints.length, message: 'Unified test generation accepted. Result will be sent to callbackUrl.' } }}
